attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- bdmg-report: Submit or update mgorabbani's daily personal report on reports.bmcag.org. Use when the user asks to log/submit/update a BMCAG / BDMG personal report, set values for fields like কুরআন অধ্যয়ন, হাদিস, নামাজ, আত্মসমালোচনা, etc. Handles login, upsert semantics, and verification.
- generate-image: Generate images using OpenAI's GPT image API. Use when the user asks to create, generate, draw, or design an image, illustration, icon, logo, mockup, or visual asset.
- code-simplifier: Use when asked to simplify recently changed code without changing behavior or explicitly asked to run the code-simplifier subagent.
- agent-skills:ship: Run the pre-launch checklist and prepare for production deployment
- agent-skills:code-simplify: Simplify code for clarity and maintainability — reduce complexity without changing behavior
- agent-skills:build: Implement the next task incrementally — build, test, verify, commit
- agent-skills:plan: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering
- agent-skills:spec: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code
- agent-skills:review: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance
- agent-skills:test: Run TDD workflow — write failing tests, implement, verify. For bugs, use the Prove-It pattern.
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- expo:upgrading-expo: Guidelines for upgrading Expo SDK versions and fixing dependency issues
- expo:expo-tailwind-setup: Set up Tailwind CSS v4 in Expo with react-native-css and NativeWind v5 for universal styling
- expo:expo-cicd-workflows: Helps understand and write EAS workflow YAML files for Expo projects. Use this skill when the user asks about CI/CD or workflows in an Expo or EAS context, mentions .eas/workflows/, or wants help with EAS build pipelines or deployment automation.
- expo:expo-deployment: Deploying Expo apps to iOS App Store, Android Play Store, web hosting, and API routes
- expo:expo-ui-swift-ui: `@expo/ui/swift-ui` package lets you use SwiftUI Views and modifiers in your app.
- expo:building-native-ui: Complete guide for building beautiful apps with Expo Router. Covers fundamentals, styling, components, navigation, animations, patterns, and native tabs.
- expo:native-data-fetching: Use when implementing or debugging ANY network request, API call, or data fetching. Covers fetch API, React Query, SWR, error handling, caching, offline support, and Expo Router data loaders (`useLoaderData`).
- expo:expo-dev-client: Build and distribute Expo development clients locally or via TestFlight
- expo:expo-ui-jetpack-compose: `@expo/ui/jetpack-compose` package lets you use Jetpack Compose Views and modifiers in your app.
- expo:use-dom: Use Expo DOM components to run web code in a webview on native and as-is on web. Migrate web code to native incrementally.
- expo:expo-api-routes: Guidelines for creating API routes in Expo Router with EAS Hosting
- agent-skills:test-driven-development: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior. Use when you need to prove that code works, when a bug report arrives, or when you're about to modify existing functionality.
- agent-skills:using-agent-skills: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or when you need to discover which skill applies to the current task. This is the meta-skill that governs how all other skills are discovered and invoked.
- agent-skills:planning-and-task-breakdown: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.
- agent-skills:incremental-implementation: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.
- agent-skills:debugging-and-error-recovery: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.
- agent-skills:idea-refine: Refines ideas iteratively. Refine ideas through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use "idea-refine" or "ideate" to trigger.
- agent-skills:deprecation-and-migration: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.
- agent-skills:security-and-hardening: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.
- agent-skills:documentation-and-adrs: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.
- agent-skills:api-and-interface-design: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.
- agent-skills:ci-cd-and-automation: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.
- agent-skills:performance-optimization: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
- agent-skills:frontend-ui-engineering: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.
- agent-skills:spec-driven-development: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.
- agent-skills:browser-testing-with-devtools: Tests in real browsers. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data via Chrome DevTools MCP.
- agent-skills:git-workflow-and-versioning: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.
- agent-skills:context-engineering: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.
- agent-skills:shipping-and-launch: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.
- agent-skills:code-simplification: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.
- agent-skills:code-review-and-quality: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.
- caveman:caveman-commit: Ultra-compressed commit message generator. Cuts noise from commit messages while preserving intent and reasoning. Conventional Commits format. Subject ≤50 chars, body only when "why" isn't obvious. Use when user says "write a commit", "commit message", "generate commit", "/commit", or invokes /caveman-commit. Auto-triggers when staging changes.
- caveman:caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by speaking like caveman while keeping full technical accuracy. Supports intensity levels: lite, full (default), ultra, wenyan-lite, wenyan-full, wenyan-ultra. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman. Also auto-triggers when token efficiency is requested.
- caveman:caveman-review: Ultra-compressed code review comments. Cuts noise from PR feedback while preserving the actionable signal. Each comment is one line: location, problem, fix. Use when user says "review this PR", "code review", "review the diff", "/review", or invokes /caveman-review. Auto-triggers when reviewing pull requests.
- caveman:caveman-help: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
- caveman:compress: Compress natural language memory files (CLAUDE.md, todos, preferences) into caveman format to save input tokens. Preserves all technical substance, code, URLs, and structure. Compressed version overwrites the original file. Human-readable backup saved as FILE.original.md. Trigger: /caveman:compress <filepath> or "compress memory file"
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from dataclasses import dataclass
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Annotated

import structlog
from fastapi import Depends

from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, Settings, get_settings
from app.schemas.webhook import IngestEvent
from app.services.classify import Classification, classify_document
from app.services.conflict import scan_patch_plan_conflicts
from app.services.extract import extract_patch_plan
from app.services.handlers import get_event_handler
from app.services.llm.client import LLMClient, get_llm_client
from app.services.locate import locate_sections
from app.services.patcher.apply import PatchApplyResult, apply_patch_plan
from app.services.patcher.atomic import atomic_write_text
from app.services.reindex import reindex_property
from app.services.resolve import resolve_context
from app.storage.stammdaten import StammdatenStore, open_stammdaten
from app.storage.wiki_chunks import open_wiki_chunks

log = structlog.get_logger(__name__)


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SupervisorResult:
    event_id: str
    status: str
    classification: Classification | None
    patch: PatchApplyResult | None


class Supervisor:
    def __init__(self, *, settings: Settings, llm: LLMClient) -> None:
        self._settings = settings
        self._llm = llm

    async def handle(self, event: IngestEvent) -> SupervisorResult:
        handler = get_event_handler(event.event_type)
        normalized = await handler.handle(event, self._settings)

        classification = await classify_document(
            normalized_text=normalized.normalized_text,
            llm=self._llm,
            settings=self._settings,
        )
        if not classification.signal:
            log.info(
                "ingest_short_circuit",
                event_id=event.event_id,
                event_type=event.event_type,
                category=classification.category,
            )
            return SupervisorResult(event.event_id, "no_signal", classification, None)

        stammdaten = _open_stammdaten(self._settings, property_id=event.property_id)
        resolution = resolve_context(
            normalized_text=normalized.normalized_text,
            stammdaten=stammdaten,
            property_id=event.property_id,
        )
        wiki_chunks_db = _wiki_chunks_db_path(self._settings)
        wiki_chunks = open_wiki_chunks(wiki_chunks_db)
        if (self._settings.wiki_dir / event.property_id).is_dir() and not wiki_chunks.has_property(
            event.property_id
        ):
            reindex_property(
                wiki_dir=self._settings.wiki_dir,
                property_id=event.property_id,
                db_path=wiki_chunks_db,
            )
            wiki_chunks = open_wiki_chunks(wiki_chunks_db)
        sections = locate_sections(
            wiki_chunks=wiki_chunks,
            property_id=event.property_id,
            entity_ids=resolution.entity_ids,
            query_text=normalized.normalized_text,
        )
        plan = await extract_patch_plan(
            event_id=event.event_id,
            event_type=event.event_type,
            property_id=event.property_id,
            normalized_text=normalized.normalized_text,
            resolution=resolution,
            sections=sections,
            llm=self._llm,
            settings=self._settings,
        )
        plan, conflict_issues = scan_patch_plan_conflicts(plan, wiki_dir=self._settings.wiki_dir)
        if conflict_issues:
            log.info(
                "ingest_conflicts_deferred",
                event_id=event.event_id,
                deferred=len(conflict_issues),
            )

        patch = apply_patch_plan(
            plan.model_dump(exclude_none=True),
            wiki_dir=self._settings.wiki_dir,
            vocabulary_path=REPO_ROOT / "schema" / "VOCABULARY.md",
            wiki_chunks_db_path=wiki_chunks_db,
        )
        return SupervisorResult(event.event_id, "applied", classification, patch)

    def record_failed_event(self, event: IngestEvent, reason: str) -> None:
        property_root = self._settings.wiki_dir / event.property_id
        property_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        path = property_root / "_hermes_feedback.jsonl"
        existing = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if path.exists() else ""
        record = {
            "kind": "ingest",
            "event_id": event.event_id,
            "property_id": event.property_id,
            "event_type": event.event_type,
            "retrieval_success": False,
            "error": reason,
        }
        atomic_write_text(path, existing + json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")


def _open_stammdaten(settings: Settings, *, property_id: str) -> StammdatenStore:
    db_path = settings.output_dir / "stammdaten.duckdb"
    db_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    store = open_stammdaten(db_path)
    if store.find_entity_by_id(property_id) is None:
        source = settings.data_dir / "stammdaten" / "stammdaten.json"
        if source.is_file():
            store.load_from_json(source)
    return store


def _wiki_chunks_db_path(settings: Settings) -> Path:
    settings.output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    return settings.output_dir / "wiki_chunks.duckdb"


def get_supervisor(
    settings: Annotated[Settings, Depends(get_settings)],
    llm: Annotated[LLMClient, Depends(get_llm_client)],
) -> Supervisor:
    return Supervisor(settings=settings, llm=llm)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	from email.utils import parseaddr
5	
6	from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, Settings
7	from app.services.llm.client import LLMClient
8	from app.services.llm.json import parse_json_object
9	
10	_MIN_TABLE_CELLS = 2
11	
12	
13	@dataclass(frozen=True)
14	class Classification:
15	    signal: bool
16	    category: str
17	    priority: str
18	    confidence: float
19	
20	
21	def classify_prompt(*, normalized_text: str) -> str:
22	    excerpt = normalized_text[:500]
23	    sender = _markdown_field(normalized_text, "From")
24	    subject = _markdown_field(normalized_text, "Subject")
25	    if sender:
26	        _, email = parseaddr(sender)
27	        sender = email or sender
28	    return (
29	        "Classify this normalized property-management source. Return only JSON with "
30	        "keys: signal (boolean), category (string), priority (low|medium|high), "
31	        "confidence (0..1).\n\n"
32	        f"Sender: {sender}\n"
33	        f"Subject: {subject}\n"
34	        f"Excerpt:\n{excerpt}"
35	    )
36	
37	
38	async def classify_document(
39	    *,
40	    normalized_text: str,
41	    llm: LLMClient,
42	    settings: Settings,
43	) -> Classification:
44	    response = await llm.complete(
45	        model=settings.fast_model,
46	        system_prompt=_classification_system_prompt(),
47	        user_prompt=classify_prompt(normalized_text=normalized_text),
48	    )
49	    payload = parse_json_object(response)
50	    return Classification(
51	        signal=bool(payload.get("signal", False)),
52	        category=str(payload.get("category", "noise")),
53	        priority=str(payload.get("priority", "low")),
54	        confidence=float(payload.get("confidence", 0.0)),
55	    )
56	
57	
58	def _classification_system_prompt() -> str:

from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass
from email.utils import parseaddr

from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, Settings
from app.services.llm.client import LLMClient
from app.services.llm.json import parse_json_object

_MIN_TABLE_CELLS = 2


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class Classification:
    signal: bool
    category: str
    priority: str
    confidence: float


def classify_prompt(*, normalized_text: str) -> str:
    excerpt = normalized_text[:500]
    sender = _markdown_field(normalized_text, "From")
    subject = _markdown_field(normalized_text, "Subject")
    if sender:
        _, email = parseaddr(sender)
        sender = email or sender
    return (
        "Classify this normalized property-management source. Return only JSON with "
        "keys: signal (boolean), category (string), priority (low|medium|high), "
        "confidence (0..1).\n\n"
        f"Sender: {sender}\n"
        f"Subject: {subject}\n"
        f"Excerpt:\n{excerpt}"
    )


async def classify_document(
    *,
    normalized_text: str,
    llm: LLMClient,
    settings: Settings,
) -> Classification:
    response = await llm.complete(
        model=settings.fast_model,
        system_prompt=_classification_system_prompt(),
        user_prompt=classify_prompt(normalized_text=normalized_text),
    )
    payload = parse_json_object(response)
    return Classification(
        signal=bool(payload.get("signal", False)),
        category=str(payload.get("category", "noise")),
        priority=str(payload.get("priority", "low")),
        confidence=float(payload.get("confidence", 0.0)),
    )


def _classification_system_prompt() -> str:
    path = REPO_ROOT / "schema" / "CLAUDE.md"
    if path.exists():
        return path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    return "You classify property-management input into signal/noise."


def _markdown_field(text: str, field: str) -> str:
    needle = f"| {field} |"
    for line in text.splitlines():
        if not line.startswith(needle):
            continue
        cells = [cell.strip().replace(r"\|", "|") for cell in line.strip().strip("|").split("|")]
        if len(cells) >= _MIN_TABLE_CELLS:
            return cells[1]
    return ""

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import re
4	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
5	from email.utils import getaddresses
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from app.storage.stammdaten import StammdatenStore
9	
10	_ENTITY_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:LIE|HAUS|EH|EIG|MIE|DL)-\d{2,5}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
11	_SOURCE_ID_RE = re.compile(r"\b(?:EMAIL|INV|LTR|TX)-[A-Z]*-?\d{3,6}\b", re.IGNORECASE)
12	_EMAIL_RE = re.compile(r"[\w.!#$%&'*+/=?^`{|}~-]+@[\w.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}")
13	_IBAN_RE = re.compile(r"\b[A-Z]{2}\d{2}(?:[ ]?[A-Z0-9]){11,30}\b")
14	
15	
16	@dataclass(frozen=True)
17	class ResolvedEntity:
18	    id: str
19	    role: str
20	    source: str
21	    data: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
22	
23	
24	@dataclass(frozen=True)
25	class ResolutionResult:
26	    property_id: str
27	    entities: list[ResolvedEntity]
28	    mentioned_ids: list[str]
29	    source_ids: list[str]
30	    unresolved_ids: list[str]
31	
32	    @property
33	    def entity_ids(self) -> list[str]:
34	        ids = [entity.id for entity in self.entities]
35	        ids.extend(self.mentioned_ids)
36	        return _dedupe(ids)
37	
38	
39	def resolve_context(
40	    *,
41	    normalized_text: str,
42	    stammdaten: StammdatenStore,
43	    property_id: str = "LIE-001",
44	) -> ResolutionResult:
45	    entities: list[ResolvedEntity] = []
46	    unresolved_ids: list[str] = []
47	
48	    for email in _emails(normalized_text):
49	        row = stammdaten.find_entity_by_email(email)
50	        if row is not None:
51	            entities.extend(_entity_chain(row, source="email", stammdaten=stammdaten))
52	
53	    for iban in _ibans(normalized_text):
54	        row = stammdaten.find_entity_by_iban(iban)
55	        if row is not None:
56	            entities.extend(_entity_chain(row, source="iban", stammdaten=stammdaten))
57	
58	    mentioned_ids = []
59	    for entity_id in _entity_ids(normalized_text):
60	        row = stammdaten.find_entity_by_id(entity_id)
61	        if row is None:
62	            unresolved_ids.append(entity_id)
63	            continue
64	        mentioned_ids.append(entity_id)
65	        entities.extend(_entity_chain(row, source="mentioned_id", stammdaten=stammdaten))
66	
67	    return ResolutionResult(
68	        property_id=property_id,
69	        entities=_dedupe_entities(entities),
70	        mentioned_ids=_dedupe(mentioned_ids),
71	        source_ids=_source_ids(normalized_text),
72	        unresolved_ids=_dedupe(unresolved_ids),
73	    )
74	
75	
76	def _emails(text: str) -> list[str]:
77	    raw_addresses = _EMAIL_RE.findall(text)
78	    parsed = [email for _, email in getaddresses(raw_addresses) if email]
79	    return _dedupe([email.lower() for email in parsed or raw_addresses])
80	
81	
82	def _ibans(text: str) -> list[str]:
83	    return _dedupe(match.group(0).replace(" ", "").upper() for match in _IBAN_RE.finditer(text))
84	
85	
86	def _entity_ids(text: str) -> list[str]:
87	    return _dedupe(match.group(0).upper() for match in _ENTITY_ID_RE.finditer(text))
88	
89	
90	def _source_ids(text: str) -> list[str]:
91	    return _dedupe(match.group(0).upper() for match in _SOURCE_ID_RE.finditer(text))
92	
93	
94	def _entity_chain(
95	    row: dict[str, Any],
96	    *,
97	    source: str,
98	    stammdaten: StammdatenStore,
99	) -> list[ResolvedEntity]:
100	    entities = [_entity_from_row(row, source)]
101	    for related_field in ("einheit_id", "eigentuemer_id", "haus_id"):
102	        related_id = row.get(related_field)
103	        if not isinstance(related_id, str) or not related_id:
104	            continue
105	        related = stammdaten.find_entity_by_id(related_id)
106	        if related is not None:
107	            entities.extend(_entity_chain(related, source=source, stammdaten=stammdaten))
108	    for unit_id in row.get("einheit_ids") or []:
109	        if not isinstance(unit_id, str):
110	            continue
111	        related = stammdaten.find_entity_by_id(unit_id)
112	        if related is not None:
113	            entities.extend(_entity_chain(related, source=source, stammdaten=stammdaten))
114	    return entities
115	
116	
117	def _entity_from_row(row: dict[str, Any], source: str) -> ResolvedEntity:
118	    entity_id = str(row["id"])
119	    role = str(row.get("role") or _role_from_id(entity_id))
120	    return ResolvedEntity(id=entity_id, role=role, source=source, data=row)
121	
122	
123	def _role_from_id(entity_id: str) -> str:
124	    prefix = entity_id.split("-", 1)[0].lower()
125	    return {
126	        "lie": "liegenschaft",
127	        "haus": "gebaeude",
128	        "eh": "einheit",
129	        "eig": "eigentuemer",
130	        "mie": "mieter",
131	        "dl": "dienstleister",
132	    }.get(prefix, "entity")
133	
134	
135	def _dedupe(values: list[str] | Any) -> list[str]:
136	    seen: set[str] = set()
137	    out: list[str] = []
138	    for value in values:
139	        text = str(value)
140	        if text in seen:
141	            continue
142	        seen.add(text)
143	        out.append(text)
144	    return out
145	
146	
147	def _dedupe_entities(entities: list[ResolvedEntity]) -> list[ResolvedEntity]:
148	    seen: set[str] = set()
149	    out: list[ResolvedEntity] = []
150	    for entity in entities:
151	        if entity.id in seen:
152	            continue
153	        seen.add(entity.id)
154	        out.append(entity)
155	    return out

31	_PROPERTY_ID_RE = re.compile(r"^LIE-\d{3}$")
32	
33	
34	@dataclass(frozen=True)
35	class PatchApplyResult:
36	    event_id: str
37	    applied_ops: int
38	    deferred_ops: int
39	    commit_sha: str | None
40	    idempotent: bool = False
41	
42	
43	def apply_patch_plan(
44	    plan: Mapping[str, Any],
45	    *,
46	    wiki_dir: Path,
47	    vocabulary_path: Path,
48	    wiki_chunks_db_path: Path | None = None,
49	) -> PatchApplyResult:
50	    event_id = str(plan["event_id"])
51	    property_id = str(plan["property_id"])
52	    if _PROPERTY_ID_RE.fullmatch(property_id) is None:
53	        raise ValueError("invalid property_id")
54	    property_root = wiki_dir / property_id
55	    feedback_path = property_root / "_hermes_feedback.jsonl"
56	
57	    if _feedback_contains_event(feedback_path, event_id):
58	        return PatchApplyResult(
59	            event_id=event_id,
60	            applied_ops=0,
61	            deferred_ops=0,
62	            commit_sha=_head_sha(wiki_dir),
63	            idempotent=True,
64	        )
65	
66	    ops = [op for op in plan.get("ops", []) if isinstance(op, dict)]
67	    vocabulary = parse_vocabulary(vocabulary_path)
68	    valid_ops, issues = validate_keyed_values(ops, vocabulary)
69	    append_pending_review(property_root, issues)
70	
71	    touched: set[Path] = set()
72	    for op in valid_ops:
73	        touched.update(_apply_one(property_root, op))
74	
75	    _append_feedback(feedback_path, plan, applied_ops=len(valid_ops), deferred_ops=len(issues))
76	    touched.add(feedback_path)
77	    if issues:
78	        touched.add(property_root / "_pending_review.md")
79	
80	    commit_sha = _git_commit(
81	        wiki_dir,
82	        message=f"ingest({event_id}): {str(plan.get('summary', 'patch')).strip()}",
83	    )
84	    if wiki_chunks_db_path is not None:
85	        _reindex_touched_files(
86	            wiki_dir=wiki_dir,
87	            property_id=property_id,
88	            property_root=property_root,
89	            touched=touched,
90	            db_path=wiki_chunks_db_path,
91	        )
92	    return PatchApplyResult(
93	        event_id=event_id,
94	        applied_ops=len(valid_ops),
95	        deferred_ops=len(issues),
96	        commit_sha=commit_sha,
97	    )
98	
99	
100	def _apply_one(property_root: Path, op: Mapping[str, Any]) -> set[Path]:
101	    op_name = str(op["op"])
102	    if op_name == "update_state":
103	        path = property_file_path(property_root, str(op.get("file", "_state.json")))
104	        state = update_state(
105	            path,
106	            updates=_mapping_or_none(op.get("updates")),
107	            counters=_mapping_or_none(op.get("counters")),
108	        )
109	        atomic_write_text(path, json.dumps(state, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
110	        return {path}
111	
112	    file_path = property_file_path(property_root, str(op["file"]))
113	    content = file_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
114	    section = str(op.get("section", ""))
115	
116	    if op_name == "upsert_bullet":
117	        content = upsert_bullet(
118	            content,
119	            section=section,
120	            key=str(op["key"]),
121	            text=str(op["text"]),
122	        )
123	    elif op_name == "delete_bullet":
124	        content = delete_bullet(content, section=section, key=str(op["key"]))
125	    elif op_name == "upsert_row":
126	        content = upsert_row(
127	            content,
128	            section=section,
129	            key=str(op["key"]),
130	            row=op["row"],
131	            header=_sequence_or_none(op.get("header")),
132	        )
133	    elif op_name == "delete_row":
134	        content = delete_row(content, section=section, key=str(op["key"]))
135	    elif op_name == "prepend_row":
136	        content = prepend_row(
137	            content,
138	            section=section,
139	            row=op["row"],
140	            header=_sequence_or_none(op.get("header")),
141	        )
142	    elif op_name == "prune_ring":
143	        content = prune_ring(content, section=section, max_rows=int(op["max_rows"]))
144	    elif op_name == "upsert_footnote":
145	        content = upsert_footnote(content, key=str(op["key"]), text=str(op["text"]))
146	    elif op_name == "gc_footnotes":
147	        content = gc_footnotes(content, ref_counts=_mapping_or_none(op.get("ref_counts")))
148	    else:
149	        raise ValueError(f"unknown patch op: {op_name}")
150	
151	    atomic_write_text(file_path, content)
152	    return {file_path}
153	
154	
155	def _sequence_or_none(value: object) -> list[object] | None:
156	    if value is None:
157	        return None
158	    if isinstance(value, list):
159	        return cast("list[object]", value)
160	    if isinstance(value, tuple):
161	        return list(value)
162	    return [value]
163	
164	
165	def _mapping_or_none(value: object) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
166	    if value is None:
167	        return None
168	    if isinstance(value, dict):
169	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", value)
170	    raise TypeError(f"expected mapping, got {type(value).__name__}")
171	
172	
173	def _append_feedback(
174	    path: Path,
175	    plan: Mapping[str, Any],
176	    *,
177	    applied_ops: int,
178	    deferred_ops: int,
179	) -> None:
180	    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
181	    existing = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if path.exists() else ""
182	    record = {
183	        "kind": "ingest",
184	        "event_id": plan["event_id"],
185	        "property_id": plan["property_id"],
186	        "summary": plan.get("summary", ""),
187	        "applied_ops": applied_ops,
188	        "deferred_ops": deferred_ops,
189	    }
190	    atomic_write_text(path, existing + json.dumps(record, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
191	
192	
193	def _feedback_contains_event(path: Path, event_id: str) -> bool:
194	    if not path.exists():
195	        return False
196	    for line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
197	        if not line.strip():
198	            continue
199	        try:
200	            record = json.loads(line)
201	        except json.JSONDecodeError:
202	            continue
203	        if record.get("event_id") == event_id:
204	            return True
205	    return False
206	
207	
208	def _git(args: list[str], cwd: Path, *, check: bool = True) -> subprocess.CompletedProcess[str]:
209	    return subprocess.run(  # noqa: S603
210	        ["git", *args],  # noqa: S607
211	        cwd=cwd,
212	        check=check,
213	        capture_output=True,
214	        text=True,
215	    )
216	
217	
218	def _git_commit(wiki_dir: Path, *, message: str) -> str | None:
219	    _git(["add", "-A"], cwd=wiki_dir)
220	    diff = _git(["diff", "--cached", "--quiet"], cwd=wiki_dir, check=False)
221	    if diff.returncode == 0:
222	        return _head_sha(wiki_dir)
223	    if diff.returncode != 1:
224	        raise subprocess.CalledProcessError(diff.returncode, diff.args, diff.stdout, diff.stderr)
225	    _git(["commit", "-m", message], cwd=wiki_dir)
226	    return _head_sha(wiki_dir)
227	
228	
229	def _head_sha(wiki_dir: Path) -> str | None:
230	    result = _git(["rev-parse", "HEAD"], cwd=wiki_dir, check=False)
231	    if result.returncode != 0:
232	        return None
233	    return result.stdout.strip()
234	
235	
236	def _reindex_touched_files(
237	    *,
238	    wiki_dir: Path,
239	    property_id: str,
240	    property_root: Path,
241	    touched: set[Path],
242	    db_path: Path,
243	) -> None:
244	    files = []
245	    for path in touched:
246	        if path.suffix != ".md":
247	            continue
248	        try:
249	            files.append(path.relative_to(property_root))
250	        except ValueError:
251	            continue
252	    if files:
253	        reindex_files(wiki_dir=wiki_dir, property_id=property_id, files=files, db_path=db_path)
254

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import json
4	import re
5	from collections.abc import Mapping, Sequence
6	from datetime import UTC, datetime
7	from pathlib import Path
8	from typing import Any
9	
10	HUMAN_NOTES_HEADING = "# Human Notes"
11	
12	
13	class PatchOperationError(ValueError):
14	    """Raised when a patch operation would touch unmanaged markdown."""
15	
16	
17	def _human_notes_start(content: str) -> int:
18	    marker = f"\n{HUMAN_NOTES_HEADING}"
19	    idx = content.find(marker)
20	    if idx == -1:
21	        if content.startswith(HUMAN_NOTES_HEADING):
22	            return 0
23	        raise PatchOperationError("missing # Human Notes boundary")
24	    return idx + 1
25	
26	
27	def _managed_content_end(content: str) -> int:
28	    return _human_notes_start(content)
29	
30	
31	def _section_bounds(content: str, section: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
32	    managed_end = _managed_content_end(content)
33	    managed = content[:managed_end]
34	    heading_re = re.compile(rf"^## {re.escape(section)}\s*$", re.MULTILINE)
35	    match = heading_re.search(managed)
36	    if match is None:
37	        raise PatchOperationError(f"section not found before # Human Notes: {section}")
38	
39	    next_heading = re.search(r"^## .*$", managed[match.end() :], re.MULTILINE)
40	    end = managed_end if next_heading is None else match.end() + next_heading.start()
41	    if end > managed_end:
42	        raise PatchOperationError("operation would cross # Human Notes boundary")
43	    return match.end(), end
44	
45	
46	def _section_lines(content: str, section: str) -> tuple[int, int, list[str]]:
47	    start, end = _section_bounds(content, section)
48	    return start, end, content[start:end].splitlines(keepends=True)
49	
50	
51	def _replace_section_lines(content: str, section: str, lines: Sequence[str]) -> str:
52	    start, end = _section_bounds(content, section)
53	    body = "".join(lines)
54	    if body and not body.endswith("\n"):
55	        body += "\n"
56	    return f"{content[:start]}{body}{content[end:]}"
57	
58	
59	def _ensure_section_spacing(lines: list[str]) -> list[str]:
60	    if not lines:
61	        return ["\n"]
62	    if lines[0].strip():
63	        lines.insert(0, "\n")
64	    if lines[-1].strip():
65	        lines.append("\n")
66	    return lines
67	
68	
69	def _strip_empty_state(lines: list[str]) -> list[str]:
70	    return [line for line in lines if not line.lstrip().startswith("_Keine ")]
71	
72	
73	def _keyed_bullet_re(key: str) -> re.Pattern[str]:
74	    return re.compile(rf"^\s*-\s+.*\*\*{re.escape(key)}:\*\*")
75	
76	
77	def _normalize_bullet(key: str, text: str) -> str:
78	    line = text.strip()
79	    if not line.startswith("- "):
80	        line = f"- **{key}:** {line}"
81	    elif f"**{key}:**" not in line:
82	        line = f"- **{key}:** {line[2:].strip()}"
83	    return f"{line}\n"
84	
85	
86	def upsert_bullet(content: str, *, section: str, key: str, text: str) -> str:
87	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
88	    lines = _strip_empty_state(lines)
89	    new_line = _normalize_bullet(key, text)
90	    keyed = _keyed_bullet_re(key)
91	
92	    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):
93	        if keyed.match(line):
94	            lines[idx] = new_line
95	            return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
96	
97	    insert_at = len(lines)
98	    while insert_at > 0 and not lines[insert_at - 1].strip():
99	        insert_at -= 1
100	    lines.insert(insert_at, new_line)
101	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
102	
103	
104	def delete_bullet(content: str, *, section: str, key: str) -> str:
105	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
106	    keyed = _keyed_bullet_re(key)
107	    lines = [line for line in lines if not keyed.match(line)]
108	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
109	
110	
111	def _normalize_cells(row: str | Sequence[object]) -> list[str]:
112	    if isinstance(row, str):
113	        stripped = row.strip().strip("|")
114	        return [cell.strip() for cell in stripped.split("|")]
115	    return [str(cell).strip() for cell in row]
116	
117	
118	def _render_row(cells: str | Sequence[object]) -> str:
119	    return "| " + " | ".join(_normalize_cells(cells)) + " |\n"
120	
121	
122	def _is_table_row(line: str) -> bool:
123	    return line.lstrip().startswith("|") and line.rstrip().endswith("|")
124	
125	
126	def _is_separator(line: str) -> bool:
127	    return bool(re.match(r"^\s*\|?\s*:?-{3,}:?\s*(\|\s*:?-{3,}:?\s*)+\|?\s*$", line))
128	
129	
130	def _table_bounds(lines: Sequence[str]) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
131	    start = None
132	    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):
133	        if _is_table_row(line):
134	            start = idx
135	            break
136	    if start is None:
137	        return None
138	    end = start
139	    while end < len(lines) and (_is_table_row(lines[end]) or not lines[end].strip()):
140	        if not _is_table_row(lines[end]) and end > start:
141	            break
142	        end += 1
143	    return start, end
144	
145	
146	def _row_key(line: str) -> str | None:
147	    if not _is_table_row(line) or _is_separator(line):
148	        return None
149	    cells = _normalize_cells(line)
150	    if not cells:
151	        return None
152	    return cells[0]
153	
154	
155	def _table_insert_index(lines: Sequence[str], start: int, end: int) -> int:
156	    if start + 1 < end and _is_separator(lines[start + 1]):
157	        return start + 2
158	    return start + 1
159	
160	
161	def upsert_row(
162	    content: str,
163	    *,
164	    section: str,
165	    key: str,
166	    row: str | Sequence[object],
167	    header: Sequence[object] | None = None,
168	) -> str:
169	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
170	    lines = _strip_empty_state(lines)
171	    rendered = _render_row(row)
172	    bounds = _table_bounds(lines)
173	
174	    if bounds is None:
175	        table = []
176	        if header is not None:
177	            header_cells = _normalize_cells(header)
178	            table.append(_render_row(header_cells))
179	            table.append(_render_row(["---"] * len(header_cells)))
180	        table.append(rendered)
181	        insert_at = len(lines)
182	        while insert_at > 0 and not lines[insert_at - 1].strip():
183	            insert_at -= 1
184	        lines[insert_at:insert_at] = table
185	        return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
186	
187	    start, end = bounds
188	    for idx in range(start, end):
189	        if _row_key(lines[idx]) == key:
190	            lines[idx] = rendered
191	            return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
192	
193	    lines.insert(end, rendered)
194	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
195	
196	
197	def delete_row(content: str, *, section: str, key: str) -> str:
198	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
199	    bounds = _table_bounds(lines)
200	    if bounds is None:
201	        return content
202	    start, end = bounds
203	    kept = [
204	        line for idx, line in enumerate(lines) if idx < start or idx >= end or _row_key(line) != key
205	    ]
206	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(kept))
207	
208	
209	def prepend_row(
210	    content: str,
211	    *,
212	    section: str,
213	    row: str | Sequence[object],
214	    header: Sequence[object] | None = None,
215	) -> str:
216	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
217	    lines = _strip_empty_state(lines)
218	    rendered = _render_row(row)
219	    bounds = _table_bounds(lines)
220	
221	    if bounds is None:
222	        table = []
223	        if header is not None:
224	            header_cells = _normalize_cells(header)
225	            table.append(_render_row(header_cells))
226	            table.append(_render_row(["---"] * len(header_cells)))
227	        table.append(rendered)
228	        insert_at = len(lines)
229	        while insert_at > 0 and not lines[insert_at - 1].strip():
230	            insert_at -= 1
231	        lines[insert_at:insert_at] = table
232	        return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
233	
234	    start, end = bounds
235	    lines.insert(_table_insert_index(lines, start, end), rendered)
236	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
237	
238	
239	def prune_ring(content: str, *, section: str, max_rows: int) -> str:
240	    if max_rows < 0:
241	        raise PatchOperationError("max_rows must be non-negative")
242	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
243	    bounds = _table_bounds(lines)
244	    if bounds is None:
245	        bullet_indexes = [idx for idx, line in enumerate(lines) if line.lstrip().startswith("- ")]
246	        for idx in reversed(bullet_indexes[max_rows:]):
247	            del lines[idx]
248	        return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
249	
250	    start, end = bounds
251	    first_data = _table_insert_index(lines, start, end)
252	    data_indexes = [
253	        idx
254	        for idx in range(first_data, end)
255	        if _is_table_row(lines[idx]) and not _is_separator(lines[idx])
256	    ]
257	    for idx in reversed(data_indexes[max_rows:]):
258	        del lines[idx]
259	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
260	
261	
262	def upsert_footnote(content: str, *, key: str, text: str) -> str:
263	    section = "Provenance"
264	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, section)
265	    new_line = f"[^{key}]: {text.strip()}\n"
266	    pattern = re.compile(rf"^\[\^{re.escape(key)}\]:")
267	    for idx, line in enumerate(lines):
268	        if pattern.match(line):
269	            lines[idx] = new_line
270	            return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
271	    insert_at = len(lines)
272	    while insert_at > 0 and not lines[insert_at - 1].strip():
273	        insert_at -= 1
274	    lines.insert(insert_at, new_line)
275	    return _replace_section_lines(content, section, _ensure_section_spacing(lines))
276	
277	
278	def gc_footnotes(content: str, *, ref_counts: Mapping[str, int] | None = None) -> str:
279	    _, _, lines = _section_lines(content, "Provenance")
280	    if ref_counts is None:
281	        start, _ = _section_bounds(content, "Provenance")
282	        before_provenance = content[:start]
283	        refs = set(re.findall(r"\[\^([^\]]+)\]", before_provenance))
284	    else:
285	        refs = {key for key, count in ref_counts.items() if count > 0}
286	
287	    kept = []
288	    footnote_re = re.compile(r"^\[\^([^\]]+)\]:")
289	    for line in lines:
290	        match = footnote_re.match(line)
291	        if match is not None and match.group(1) not in refs:
292	            continue
293	        kept.append(line)
294	    return _replace_section_lines(content, "Provenance", _ensure_section_spacing(kept))
295	
296	
297	def update_state(
298	    state_path: Path,
299	    *,
300	    updates: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
301	    counters: Mapping[str, int] | None = None,
302	) -> dict[str, Any]:
303	    state = json.loads(state_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
304	    for key, value in (updates or {}).items():
305	        state[key] = value
306	    if counters:
307	        counts = state.setdefault("counts", {})
308	        for key, delta in counters.items():
309	            counts[key] = int(counts.get(key, 0)) + delta
310	    state["last_patched"] = datetime.now(UTC).isoformat()
311	    return state
312

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import re
4	from dataclasses import dataclass
5	from datetime import date
6	from decimal import Decimal, InvalidOperation
7	from pathlib import Path
8	from typing import Any
9	
10	from app.schemas.patch_plan import PatchPlan
11	from app.services.patcher.pending_review import append_entries as _append_pending_entries
12	
13	_DATE_RE = re.compile(r"\b(20\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{2})\b")
14	_MONEY_RE = re.compile(r"(?:€\s*)?(-?\d+(?:[.\s]\d{3})*(?:,\d{2}|\.\d{2}))\s*(?:€|EUR)?")
15	_BULLET_STATUS_RE = re.compile(r"^\s*-\s*(?P<status>[🔴🟡🟢])")
16	
17	
18	@dataclass(frozen=True)
19	class ConflictIssue:
20	    file: str
21	    section: str
22	    key: str
23	    reason: str
24	    existing: str
25	    incoming: str
26	
27	
28	def scan_patch_plan_conflicts(
29	    plan: PatchPlan,
30	    *,
31	    wiki_dir: Path,
32	    date_drift_days: int = 3,
33	    amount_delta_ratio: Decimal = Decimal("0.10"),
34	) -> tuple[PatchPlan, list[ConflictIssue]]:
35	    kept_ops: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
36	    issues: list[ConflictIssue] = []
37	    property_root = wiki_dir / plan.property_id
38	
39	    for op in plan.ops:
40	        op_data = op.model_dump(exclude_none=True)
41	        issue = _conflict_for_op(
42	            op_data,
43	            property_root=property_root,
44	            date_drift_days=date_drift_days,
45	            amount_delta_ratio=amount_delta_ratio,
46	        )
47	        if issue is None:
48	            kept_ops.append(op_data)
49	        else:
50	            issues.append(issue)
51	
52	    if issues:
53	        append_conflicts(property_root, issues)
54	
55	    data = plan.model_dump()
56	    data["ops"] = kept_ops
57	    return PatchPlan.model_validate(data), issues
58	
59	
60	def append_conflicts(property_root: Path, issues: list[ConflictIssue]) -> None:
61	    if not issues:
62	        return
63	    entries = [
64	        "\n".join(
65	            [
66	                "",
67	                f"### conflict: {issue.key}",
68	                f"- file: `{issue.file}`",
69	                f"- section: `{issue.section}`",
70	                f"- reason: {issue.reason}",
71	                f"- existing: `{issue.existing}`",
72	                f"- incoming: `{issue.incoming}`",
73	                "",
74	            ]
75	        )
76	        for issue in issues
77	    ]
78	    _append_pending_entries(property_root / "_pending_review.md", entries)
79	
80	
81	def _conflict_for_op(
82	    op: dict[str, Any],
83	    *,
84	    property_root: Path,
85	    date_drift_days: int,
86	    amount_delta_ratio: Decimal,
87	) -> ConflictIssue | None:
88	    if op.get("op") not in {"upsert_bullet", "upsert_row"}:
89	        return None
90	    file = str(op.get("file", ""))
91	    section = str(op.get("section", ""))
92	    key = str(op.get("key", ""))
93	    if not file or not section or not key:
94	        return None
95	    path = property_root / file
96	    if not path.is_file():
97	        return None
98	    existing = _existing_keyed_line(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"), section=section, key=key)
99	    if existing is None:
100	        return None
101	    incoming = _incoming_line(op)
102	    reason = _conflict_reason(
103	        existing,
104	        incoming,
105	        date_drift_days=date_drift_days,
106	        amount_delta_ratio=amount_delta_ratio,
107	    )
108	    if reason is None:
109	        return None
110	    return ConflictIssue(
111	        file=file,
112	        section=section,
113	        key=key,
114	        reason=reason,
115	        existing=existing,
116	        incoming=incoming,
117	    )
118	
119	
120	def _existing_keyed_line(content: str, *, section: str, key: str) -> str | None:
121	    body = _section_body(content, section)
122	    if body is None:
123	        return None
124	    bullet_key = re.compile(rf"\*\*{re.escape(key)}:\*\*")
125	    for line in body.splitlines():
126	        if line.lstrip().startswith("- ") and bullet_key.search(line):
127	            return line.strip()
128	        if line.lstrip().startswith("|") and _row_key(line) == key:
129	            return line.strip()
130	    return None
131	
132	
133	def _section_body(content: str, section: str) -> str | None:
134	    managed = content.split("\n# Human Notes", 1)[0]
135	    match = re.search(rf"^## {re.escape(section)}\s*$", managed, flags=re.MULTILINE)
136	    if match is None:
137	        return None
138	    next_match = re.search(r"^## .*$", managed[match.end() :], flags=re.MULTILINE)
139	    end = len(managed) if next_match is None else match.end() + next_match.start()
140	    return managed[match.end() : end]
141	
142	
143	def _row_key(line: str) -> str | None:
144	    cells = [cell.strip() for cell in line.strip().strip("|").split("|")]
145	    if not cells or set(cells[0]) <= {"-"}:
146	        return None
147	    return cells[0]
148	
149	
150	def _incoming_line(op: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
151	    if op.get("op") == "upsert_bullet":
152	        text = str(op.get("text") or op.get("content") or "")
153	        if text.lstrip().startswith("- "):
154	            return text.strip()
155	        key = str(op.get("key", ""))
156	        return f"- **{key}:** {text.strip()}"
157	    row = op.get("row") or op.get("content") or ""
158	    if isinstance(row, list):
159	        return "| " + " | ".join(str(cell).strip() for cell in row) + " |"
160	    return str(row).strip()
161	
162	
163	def _conflict_reason(
164	    existing: str,
165	    incoming: str,
166	    *,
167	    date_drift_days: int,
168	    amount_delta_ratio: Decimal,
169	) -> str | None:
170	    old_status = _status(existing)
171	    new_status = _status(incoming)
172	    if old_status == "🔴" and new_status == "🟢":
173	        return "status flip requires human approval"
174	
175	    old_date = _first_date(existing)
176	    new_date = _first_date(incoming)
177	    if (
178	        old_date is not None
179	        and new_date is not None
180	        and abs((new_date - old_date).days) > date_drift_days
181	    ):
182	        return f"date drift exceeds {date_drift_days} days"
183	
184	    old_amount = _first_amount(existing)
185	    new_amount = _first_amount(incoming)
186	    if old_amount is not None and new_amount is not None and old_amount != 0:
187	        delta = abs(new_amount - old_amount) / abs(old_amount)
188	        if delta > amount_delta_ratio:
189	            return f"amount delta exceeds {amount_delta_ratio:.0%}"
190	    return None
191	
192	
193	def _status(line: str) -> str | None:
194	    match = _BULLET_STATUS_RE.match(line)
195	    return match.group("status") if match is not None else None
196	
197	
198	def _first_date(line: str) -> date | None:
199	    match = _DATE_RE.search(line)
200	    if match is None:
201	        return None
202	    try:
203	        return date.fromisoformat(match.group(1))
204	    except ValueError:
205	        return None
206	
207	
208	def _first_amount(line: str) -> Decimal | None:
209	    for match in _MONEY_RE.finditer(line):
210	        raw = match.group(1).replace(" ", "")
211	        if raw.count(",") == 1:
212	            raw = raw.replace(".", "").replace(",", ".")
213	        try:
214	            return Decimal(raw)
215	        except InvalidOperation:
216	            continue
217	    return None
218

52	    *,

84	def canonicalize_patch_plan(

89	    event_type: str,
90	    source_ids: list[str] | None = None,
91	) -> PatchPlan:
92	    data = dict(payload)
93	    data["event_id"] = event_id
94	    data["property_id"] = property_id
95	    data["event_type"] = event_type
96	    if source_ids and not data.get("source_ids"):
97	        data["source_ids"] = source_ids
98	    data["ops"] = [_canonical_op(op, property_id=property_id) for op in data.get("ops", [])]
99	    return PatchPlan.model_validate(data)
100	
101	
102	def _canonical_op(raw: object, *, property_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
103	    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
104	        raise ValueError("patch op must be an object")
105	    op = dict(raw)
106	    op_name = str(op.get("op", ""))
107	    if "file" in op and op["file"] is not None:
108	        op["file"] = _normalize_file(str(op["file"]), property_id=property_id)
109	
110	    if op_name == "upsert_bullet" and "text" not in op and "content" in op:
111	        op["text"] = op["content"]
112	    if op_name in {"upsert_row", "prepend_row"} and "row" not in op and "content" in op:
113	        op["row"] = op["content"]
114	    if op_name == "upsert_footnote" and "text" not in op and "value" in op:
115	        op["text"] = op["value"]
116	    if op_name == "update_state" and "updates" not in op and "field" in op:
117	        op["updates"] = {str(op["field"]): op.get("value")}
118	    return op
119	
120	
121	def _normalize_file(path: str, *, property_id: str) -> str:
122	    return normalize_property_file(path, property_id=property_id)
123	
124	
125	def _extract_system_prompt() -> str:
126	    parts = []
127	    for relative in (
128	        "schema/CLAUDE.md",
129	        "schema/VOCABULARY.md",
130	        "schema/extractors/00_shared_rules.md",
131	    ):
132	        path = REPO_ROOT / relative
133	        if path.exists():
134	            parts.append(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
135	    return "\n\n".join(parts) or "You produce PatchPlan JSON for a markdown wiki patcher."

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from typing import Any

from app.core.config import REPO_ROOT, Settings
from app.schemas.patch_plan import PatchPlan
from app.services.llm.client import LLMClient
from app.services.llm.json import parse_json_object
from app.services.locate import LocatedSection
from app.services.patcher.paths import normalize_property_file
from app.services.resolve import ResolutionResult


def extract_prompt(
    *,
    event_id: str,
    event_type: str,
    property_id: str,
    normalized_text: str,
    resolution: ResolutionResult,
    sections: list[LocatedSection],
) -> str:
    located = [
        {
            "file": section.file,
            "section": section.section,
            "entity_refs": section.entity_refs,
            "body": section.body,
        }
        for section in sections
    ]
    payload = {
        "event_id": event_id,
        "event_type": event_type,
        "property_id": property_id,
        "resolved_entity_ids": resolution.entity_ids,
        "source_ids": resolution.source_ids,
        "unresolved_ids": resolution.unresolved_ids,
        "located_sections": located,
        "normalized_document": normalized_text,
    }
    return (
        "Produce one PatchPlan JSON object for this normalized source. "
        "Use only the provided located sections and schema vocabulary. "
        "Return JSON only, no markdown.\n\n"
        f"{json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, default=str)}"
    )


async def extract_patch_plan(
    *,
    event_id: str,
    event_type: str,
    property_id: str,
    normalized_text: str,
    resolution: ResolutionResult,
    sections: list[LocatedSection],
    llm: LLMClient,
    settings: Settings,
) -> PatchPlan:
    response = await llm.complete(
        model=settings.smart_model,
        system_prompt=_extract_system_prompt(),
        user_prompt=extract_prompt(
            event_id=event_id,
            event_type=event_type,
            property_id=property_id,
            normalized_text=normalized_text,
            resolution=resolution,
            sections=sections,
        ),
    )
    payload = parse_json_object(response)
    return canonicalize_patch_plan(
        payload,
        event_id=event_id,
        property_id=property_id,
        event_type=event_type,
        source_ids=resolution.source_ids,
    )


def canonicalize_patch_plan(
    payload: dict[str, Any],
    *,
    event_id: str,
    property_id: str,
    event_type: str,
    source_ids: list[str] | None = None,
) -> PatchPlan:
    data = dict(payload)
    data["event_id"] = event_id
    data["property_id"] = property_id
    data["event_type"] = event_type
    if source_ids and not data.get("source_ids"):
        data["source_ids"] = source_ids
    data["ops"] = [_canonical_op(op, property_id=property_id) for op in data.get("ops", [])]
    return PatchPlan.model_validate(data)


def _canonical_op(raw: object, *, property_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        raise ValueError("patch op must be an object")
    op = dict(raw)
    op_name = str(op.get("op", ""))
    if "file" in op and op["file"] is not None:
        op["file"] = _normalize_file(str(op["file"]), property_id=property_id)

    if op_name == "upsert_bullet" and "text" not in op and "content" in op:
        op["text"] = op["content"]
    if op_name in {"upsert_row", "prepend_row"} and "row" not in op and "content" in op:
        op["row"] = op["content"]
    if op_name == "upsert_footnote" and "text" not in op and "value" in op:
        op["text"] = op["value"]
    if op_name == "update_state" and "updates" not in op and "field" in op:
        op["updates"] = {str(op["field"]): op.get("value")}
    return op


def _normalize_file(path: str, *, property_id: str) -> str:
    return normalize_property_file(path, property_id=property_id)


def _extract_system_prompt() -> str:
    parts = []
    for relative in (
        "schema/CLAUDE.md",
        "schema/VOCABULARY.md",
        "schema/extractors/00_shared_rules.md",
    ):
        path = REPO_ROOT / relative
        if path.exists():
            parts.append(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    return "\n\n".join(parts) or "You produce PatchPlan JSON for a markdown wiki patcher."

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	from dataclasses import dataclass
4	from typing import Any
5	
6	from app.storage.wiki_chunks import WikiChunksStore
7	
8	
9	@dataclass(frozen=True)
10	class LocatedSection:
11	    file: str
12	    section: str
13	    body: str
14	    entity_refs: list[str]
15	    score: float = 0.0
16	
17	
18	def locate_sections(
19	    *,
20	    wiki_chunks: WikiChunksStore,
21	    property_id: str,
22	    entity_ids: list[str],
23	    query_text: str = "",
24	    limit: int = 8,
25	) -> list[LocatedSection]:
26	    wiki_chunks.build_index()
27	    found: dict[tuple[str, str], LocatedSection] = {}
28	
29	    for entity_id in entity_ids:
30	        for row in wiki_chunks.find_by_entity(property_id, entity_id):
31	            key = (str(row["file"]), str(row["section"]))
32	            found[key] = LocatedSection(
33	                file=str(row["file"]),
34	                section=str(row["section"]),
35	                body=str(row["body"]),
36	                entity_refs=list(row.get("entity_refs") or []),
37	                score=max(found.get(key, _empty_section()).score, 10.0),
38	            )
39	            if len(found) >= limit:
40	                return list(found.values())[:limit]
41	
42	    if query_text:
43	        for row in wiki_chunks.query(query_text, property_id=property_id, limit=limit):
44	            key = (str(row["file"]), str(row["section"]))
45	            if key in found:
46	                continue
47	            found[key] = LocatedSection(
48	                file=str(row["file"]),
49	                section=str(row["section"]),
50	                body=str(row["body"]),
51	                entity_refs=list(_refs(row)),
52	                score=float(row.get("score", 0.0)),
53	            )
54	            if len(found) >= limit:
55	                break
56	
57	    return list(found.values())[:limit]
58	
59	
60	def _refs(row: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
61	    refs = row.get("entity_refs", [])
62	    if isinstance(refs, list):
63	        return [str(ref) for ref in refs]
64	    return []
65	
66	
67	def _empty_section() -> LocatedSection:
68	    return LocatedSection(file="", section="", body="", entity_refs=[], score=0.0)
69

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import duckdb

from app.storage.db import connect

_TABLES: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "liegenschaft",
    "gebaeude",
    "einheiten",
    "eigentuemer",
    "mieter",
    "dienstleister",
)

_SCHEMA_SQL: tuple[str, ...] = (
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS liegenschaft (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        name VARCHAR,
        strasse VARCHAR,
        plz VARCHAR,
        ort VARCHAR,
        baujahr INTEGER,
        sanierung INTEGER,
        verwalter VARCHAR,
        verwalter_strasse VARCHAR,
        verwalter_plz VARCHAR,
        verwalter_ort VARCHAR,
        verwalter_email VARCHAR,
        verwalter_telefon VARCHAR,
        verwalter_iban VARCHAR,
        verwalter_bic VARCHAR,
        verwalter_bank VARCHAR,
        verwalter_steuernummer VARCHAR,
        weg_bankkonto_iban VARCHAR,
        weg_bankkonto_bic VARCHAR,
        weg_bankkonto_bank VARCHAR,
        ruecklage_iban VARCHAR,
        ruecklage_bic VARCHAR
    )
    """,
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS gebaeude (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        hausnr VARCHAR,
        einheiten INTEGER,
        etagen INTEGER,
        fahrstuhl BOOLEAN,
        baujahr INTEGER
    )
    """,
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS einheiten (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        haus_id VARCHAR,
        einheit_nr VARCHAR,
        lage VARCHAR,
        typ VARCHAR,
        wohnflaeche_qm DOUBLE,
        zimmer DOUBLE,
        miteigentumsanteil INTEGER
    )
    """,
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eigentuemer (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        anrede VARCHAR,
        vorname VARCHAR,
        nachname VARCHAR,
        firma VARCHAR,
        strasse VARCHAR,
        plz VARCHAR,
        ort VARCHAR,
        land VARCHAR,
        email VARCHAR,
        telefon VARCHAR,
        iban VARCHAR,
        bic VARCHAR,
        einheit_ids VARCHAR[],
        selbstnutzer BOOLEAN,
        sev_mandat BOOLEAN,
        beirat BOOLEAN,
        sprache VARCHAR
    )
    """,
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS mieter (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        anrede VARCHAR,
        vorname VARCHAR,
        nachname VARCHAR,
        email VARCHAR,
        telefon VARCHAR,
        einheit_id VARCHAR,
        eigentuemer_id VARCHAR,
        mietbeginn DATE,
        mietende DATE,
        kaltmiete DOUBLE,
        nk_vorauszahlung DOUBLE,
        kaution DOUBLE,
        iban VARCHAR,
        bic VARCHAR,
        sprache VARCHAR
    )
    """,
    """
    CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dienstleister (
        id VARCHAR PRIMARY KEY,
        firma VARCHAR,
        branche VARCHAR,
        ansprechpartner VARCHAR,
        email VARCHAR,
        telefon VARCHAR,
        strasse VARCHAR,
        plz VARCHAR,
        ort VARCHAR,
        land VARCHAR,
        iban VARCHAR,
        bic VARCHAR,
        ust_id VARCHAR,
        steuernummer VARCHAR,
        stil VARCHAR,
        sprache VARCHAR,
        vertrag_monatlich DOUBLE,
        stundensatz DOUBLE
    )
    """,
)

_INDEX_SQL: tuple[str, ...] = (
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eigentuemer_email ON eigentuemer(email)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eigentuemer_iban ON eigentuemer(iban)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mieter_email ON mieter(email)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mieter_iban ON mieter(iban)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dienstleister_email ON dienstleister(email)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_dienstleister_iban ON dienstleister(iban)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_einheiten_haus ON einheiten(haus_id)",
    "CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mieter_einheit ON mieter(einheit_id)",
)

_ID_PREFIX_TO_TABLE: dict[str, str] = {
    "MIE": "mieter",
    "EIG": "eigentuemer",
    "DL": "dienstleister",
    "EH": "einheiten",
    "HAUS": "gebaeude",
    "LIE": "liegenschaft",
}

_TABLE_TO_ROLE: dict[str, str] = {
    "mieter": "mieter",
    "eigentuemer": "eigentuemer",
    "dienstleister": "dienstleister",
}


def _normalize_iban(value: str | None) -> str | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    return value.replace(" ", "").upper()


def _row_to_dict(
    conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection, table: str, row: tuple[Any, ...]
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    columns = [c[0] for c in conn.execute(f"DESCRIBE {table}").fetchall()]
    return dict(zip(columns, row, strict=False))


class StammdatenStore:
    def __init__(self, conn: duckdb.DuckDBPyConnection) -> None:
        self._conn = conn

    def init_schema(self) -> None:
        for stmt in _SCHEMA_SQL:
            self._conn.execute(stmt)
        for stmt in _INDEX_SQL:
            self._conn.execute(stmt)

    def load_from_json(self, json_path: Path) -> None:
        payload = json.loads(json_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))

        for table in _TABLES:
            self._conn.execute(f"DELETE FROM {table}")  # noqa: S608

        liegenschaft = payload.get("liegenschaft")
        if liegenschaft:
            self._insert_liegenschaft(liegenschaft)

        for row in payload.get("gebaeude", []):
            self._insert_gebaeude(row)

        for row in payload.get("einheiten", []):
            self._insert_einheit(row)

        for row in payload.get("eigentuemer", []):
            self._insert_eigentuemer(row)

        for row in payload.get("mieter", []):
            self._insert_mieter(row)

        for row in payload.get("dienstleister", []):
            self._insert_dienstleister(row)

    def find_entity_by_email(self, email: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        needle = email.strip().lower()
        for table in ("mieter", "eigentuemer", "dienstleister"):
            row = self._conn.execute(
                f"SELECT * FROM {table} WHERE LOWER(email) = ?",  # noqa: S608
                [needle],
            ).fetchone()
            if row is not None:
                return self._enrich(table, row)
        return None

    def find_entity_by_iban(self, iban: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        needle = _normalize_iban(iban)
        if needle is None:
            return None
        for table in ("mieter", "eigentuemer", "dienstleister"):
            row = self._conn.execute(
                f"SELECT * FROM {table} WHERE iban = ?",  # noqa: S608
                [needle],
            ).fetchone()
            if row is not None:
                return self._enrich(table, row)
        return None

    def find_entity_by_id(self, entity_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        if not entity_id or "-" not in entity_id:
            return None
        prefix = entity_id.split("-", 1)[0].upper()
        table = _ID_PREFIX_TO_TABLE.get(prefix)
        if table is None:
            return None
        row = self._conn.execute(
            f"SELECT * FROM {table} WHERE id = ?",  # noqa: S608
            [entity_id],
        ).fetchone()
        if row is None:
            return None
        return self._enrich(table, row)

    def _enrich(self, table: str, row: tuple[Any, ...]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        data = _row_to_dict(self._conn, table, row)
        role = _TABLE_TO_ROLE.get(table)
        if role is not None:
            data["role"] = role
        return data

    def _insert_liegenschaft(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO liegenschaft (
                id, name, strasse, plz, ort, baujahr, sanierung, verwalter,
                verwalter_strasse, verwalter_plz, verwalter_ort, verwalter_email,
                verwalter_telefon, verwalter_iban, verwalter_bic, verwalter_bank,
                verwalter_steuernummer, weg_bankkonto_iban, weg_bankkonto_bic,
                weg_bankkonto_bank, ruecklage_iban, ruecklage_bic
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("name"),
                row.get("strasse"),
                row.get("plz"),
                row.get("ort"),
                row.get("baujahr"),
                row.get("sanierung"),
                row.get("verwalter"),
                row.get("verwalter_strasse"),
                row.get("verwalter_plz"),
                row.get("verwalter_ort"),
                row.get("verwalter_email"),
                row.get("verwalter_telefon"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("verwalter_iban")),
                row.get("verwalter_bic"),
                row.get("verwalter_bank"),
                row.get("verwalter_steuernummer"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("weg_bankkonto_iban")),
                row.get("weg_bankkonto_bic"),
                row.get("weg_bankkonto_bank"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("ruecklage_iban")),
                row.get("ruecklage_bic"),
            ],
        )

    def _insert_gebaeude(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO gebaeude (id, hausnr, einheiten, etagen, fahrstuhl, baujahr)
            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("hausnr"),
                row.get("einheiten"),
                row.get("etagen"),
                row.get("fahrstuhl"),
                row.get("baujahr"),
            ],
        )

    def _insert_einheit(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO einheiten (
                id, haus_id, einheit_nr, lage, typ, wohnflaeche_qm, zimmer, miteigentumsanteil
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("haus_id"),
                row.get("einheit_nr"),
                row.get("lage"),
                row.get("typ"),
                row.get("wohnflaeche_qm"),
                row.get("zimmer"),
                row.get("miteigentumsanteil"),
            ],
        )

    def _insert_eigentuemer(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO eigentuemer (
                id, anrede, vorname, nachname, firma, strasse, plz, ort, land,
                email, telefon, iban, bic, einheit_ids, selbstnutzer, sev_mandat,
                beirat, sprache
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("anrede"),
                row.get("vorname"),
                row.get("nachname"),
                row.get("firma"),
                row.get("strasse"),
                row.get("plz"),
                row.get("ort"),
                row.get("land"),
                row.get("email"),
                row.get("telefon"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("iban")),
                row.get("bic"),
                list(row.get("einheit_ids") or []),
                row.get("selbstnutzer"),
                row.get("sev_mandat"),
                row.get("beirat"),
                row.get("sprache"),
            ],
        )

    def _insert_mieter(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO mieter (
                id, anrede, vorname, nachname, email, telefon, einheit_id,
                eigentuemer_id, mietbeginn, mietende, kaltmiete, nk_vorauszahlung,
                kaution, iban, bic, sprache
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("anrede"),
                row.get("vorname"),
                row.get("nachname"),
                row.get("email"),
                row.get("telefon"),
                row.get("einheit_id"),
                row.get("eigentuemer_id"),
                row.get("mietbeginn"),
                row.get("mietende"),
                row.get("kaltmiete"),
                row.get("nk_vorauszahlung"),
                row.get("kaution"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("iban")),
                row.get("bic"),
                row.get("sprache"),
            ],
        )

    def _insert_dienstleister(self, row: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        self._conn.execute(
            """
            INSERT INTO dienstleister (
                id, firma, branche, ansprechpartner, email, telefon, strasse,
                plz, ort, land, iban, bic, ust_id, steuernummer, stil, sprache,
                vertrag_monatlich, stundensatz
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            """,
            [
                row.get("id"),
                row.get("firma"),
                row.get("branche"),
                row.get("ansprechpartner"),
                row.get("email"),
                row.get("telefon"),
                row.get("strasse"),
                row.get("plz"),
                row.get("ort"),
                row.get("land"),
                _normalize_iban(row.get("iban")),
                row.get("bic"),
                row.get("ust_id"),
                row.get("steuernummer"),
                row.get("stil"),
                row.get("sprache"),
                row.get("vertrag_monatlich"),
                row.get("stundensatz"),
            ],
        )


def open_stammdaten(db_path: Path) -> StammdatenStore:
    conn = connect(db_path)
    store = StammdatenStore(conn)
    store.init_schema()
    return store

from __future__ import annotations

from app.services.handlers.bank import BankHandler
from app.services.handlers.base import EventHandler
from app.services.handlers.chat import ChatHandler
from app.services.handlers.document import DocumentHandler
from app.services.handlers.email import EmailHandler
from app.services.handlers.erp import ErpHandler
from app.services.handlers.invoice import InvoiceHandler
from app.services.handlers.letter import LetterHandler
from app.services.handlers.lint import LintHandler
from app.services.handlers.manual import ManualHandler
from app.services.handlers.schedule import ScheduleHandler
from app.services.handlers.voicenote import VoiceNoteHandler

_HANDLERS: dict[str, EventHandler] = {
    "email": EmailHandler(),
    "eml": EmailHandler(),
    "letter": LetterHandler(),
    "invoice": InvoiceHandler(),
    "bank": BankHandler(),
    "chat": ChatHandler(),
    "slack": ChatHandler(),
    "whatsapp": ChatHandler(),
    "voicenote": VoiceNoteHandler(),
    "erp": ErpHandler(),
    "document": DocumentHandler(),
    "manual": ManualHandler(),
    "schedule": ScheduleHandler(),
    "lint": LintHandler(),
}


def get_event_handler(event_type: str) -> EventHandler:
    return _HANDLERS.get(event_type.lower(), DocumentHandler())


__all__ = ["EventHandler", "get_event_handler"]
